name: Illinois (United States)
description: A white field bearing the Great Seal of Illinois — a bald eagle on a boulder, holding a shield with the U.S. flag and a streamer reading 'STATE SOVEREIGNTY, NATIONAL UNION' — with the word 'ILLINOIS' in blue beneath the seal.
history: The original Illinois flag, designed by Lucy Derwent and adopted on 6 July 1915, depicted only the state seal on a white field. Chief Petty Officer Bruce McDaniel, an Illinoisan serving in Vietnam, observed that no one recognized the seal-only flag, and he urged the state to add the name. The redesigned flag, with 'ILLINOIS' added beneath the seal, was adopted on 1 July 1970.

features.symbolism: a bald eagle perched on a boulder holds a shield bearing the U.S. flag and a banner reading 'STATE SOVEREIGNTY, NATIONAL UNION'; dates 1818 (statehood) and 1868 (current seal) appear on the rock
added in 1970 so the flag could be identified when seen by Illinois servicemembers in Vietnam
